phenomenon: differential visual degradation by emotion category
claim: peripheral visual processing degrades asymmetrically depending on emotional content, suggesting distinct neural pathways for processing different affective categories.

phenomenon: broad-spectrum psychological inoculation through active pattern recognition
claim: experiential learning of deceptive-communication tactics confers measurable resistance to multiple misinformation types irrespective of demographic background.
spontaneous face judgments are reducible to two independent neural-computational dimensions optimized for inferring approach-safety and capability-to-harm.
the syntactic parsing system obligatorily forces interpretations that prevent detection of elementary repetition patterns, functioning like visual illusions in blocking simpler percepts.

phenomenon: social rank-driven unethical behavior via greed-value change
claim: higher rank systematically increases unethical behavior through internalization of greed as a legitimate value, not through differential ability.

phenomenon: rational Bayesian sentence repair under noise
claim: comprehenders rationally combine prior semantic expectations with likelihood models of linguistic corruption, deviating from literal interpretation predictably by edit-distance and noise-rate.
